name: GDD/BA - SAP Production Consultant (M/F/D)
description:
GDD/BA - SAP Production Consultant (M/F/D) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GDD/BA - SAP Production Consultant (M/F/D) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now What you can expect In Manufacturing Integration and Management, we are globally responsible for the Production processes and their implementations in the SAP landscape of BASF. Furthermore, we connect underlying commercial and proprietary Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES). In addition, we provide an own inhouse MES solution. The corresponding end-to-end solutions are rolled out to our internal business partners globally in corresponding projects. Our product Manufacturing Management delivers horizontal and vertical integrated solutions from SAP to various Manufacturing Execution Systems by developing and implementing efficient production processes focusing on production development, production planning, production execution and batch management. You will join our exciting global BASF S/4 business transformation using state of the art SAP and AI solutions. Your main responsibilities: Being part of a diverse global team and working with colleagues on complex IT projects in the Manufacturing area delivering crucial solutions to businesses, functions, and operations across BASF Consulting, implementing, and supporting the full lifecycle of our products globally with focus on SAP PP & PP/PI Leading IT project manufacturing workstreams and performing all required workstream tasks from requirement engineering till after go live tasks Understand and analyze business requirements and translate them to system solutions by conducting functional design, development, deployment, modelling and configuring of the system as well as setting up master data. Willingness to learn, adopt and drive to new technologies and tools Ensure close team collaboration to leverage available knowledge sessions. Identifying and implementing improvements in each project and advising the management team in an emphatic and competent manner. Actively pull and take over tasks and work self-organized. Our main requirements: A university degree in the area of computer science or similar Several years of experience as IT consulting for IT projects in production environment for chemical or processing industry, with strong customer focus. Extended expertise as SAP PP & PP/PI consultant is a must (PP/PI must include expertise also in PP planning ). Ability to focus on goals/results by getting things done and drive them forward. Convincing and competent in customer interactions. Ability to design and implement complex business requirements. In-depth process and technical knowledge in SAP Manufacturing, preferred in an S/4 environment. Excellent communication skills in English Nice to have: Experience working with Level 3 solutions - MES integrations Experience with agile development methods like Kanban. Process knowledge in other SAP areas like QM, MM Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a

You are a trusted contact person for all questions related to charging and, together with the team, responsible for ensuring the monthly service charging through the operation and further development of the system landscape used.  This also includes carrying out, coordinating, and troubleshooting the regular monthly global service charging of Global Digital Services within the BASF Group. You communicate in a goaloriented manner with key stakeholders inside and outside of Controlling to ensure monthly charging.   You are familiar with the endtoend chain of all systems involved in the charging process.   You support the development and implementation of strategic measures, special initiatives, and the management of correct service charging.   Additionally, you identify optimization potential in charging, processes, and systems, propose improvements, and implement them independently.   You possess a comprehensive understanding of management requirements and consider them in your daily work. Qualifications: `You successfully completed your studies in business informatics with a focus on data models, ERP, or controlling, or have a comparable qualification.   You have strong IT affinity, ideally experience with SQL and BI tools.   You have strong analytical problemsolving skills and a pronounced affinity for numbers.   You have solid experience translating business requirements into program code, preferably with PL/SQL experience, Oracle; Linux shell scripts are also familiar to you.   You are familiar with web services, Java, NetBeans, KAFKA, and modern programming languages.   Writing complex SQL queries and optimizing SQL queries is nothing new to you.   You have professional experience with data analysis software and databases.   You have experience in controlling/accounting, ideally in internal service charging. What we offer: Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence.  [to include specific local benefits]. description:
GD/C - Finance Manager (M/F/D) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GD/C - Finance Manager (M/F/D) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Finance Manager Responsibilities: `We are establishing a Global Capability Center (GCC) in Hyderabad to support our company’s digitalization initiatives. As part of this strategic expansion, we seek a highly skilled and experienced Finance, Controlling & Tax Manager to manage all financial operations, statutory compliance, and tax-related matters for the GCC. This is a critical role requiring a self-driven professional who can independently handle end-to-end finance and compliance responsibilities. Key Responsibilities 1. Financial Management • Oversee all financial operations for the GCC, including budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning. • Ensure accurate and timely preparation of financial statements in compliance with local GAAP and corporate standards. • Manage accounts payable, accounts receivable, and general ledger activities. • Monitor cash flow, working capital, and liquidity requirements. • Implement internal controls to safeguard company assets and ensure financial integrity. 2. Controlling & Reporting • Develop and maintain cost control systems to monitor operational expenses. • Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports for corporate headquarters. • Conduct variance analysis and provide actionable insights to management. • Support digitalization projects with cost-benefit analysis and ROI calculations. • Ensure compliance with corporate reporting standards and timelines. 3. Taxation & Compliance • Manage all direct and indirect tax obligations, including GST, income tax, and withholding tax. • Ensure timely filing of tax returns and adherence to statutory deadlines. • Liaise with tax authorities and external consultants for audits and assessments. • Stay updated on changes in tax laws and implement necessary adjustments. • Handle transfer pricing documentation and compliance for intercompany transactions. 4. Regulatory & Statutory Compliance • Ensure compliance with Companies Act, labor laws, and other applicable regulations. • Coordinate statutory audits and internal audits; address audit observations promptly. • Maintain accurate records for regulatory inspections and filings. 5. Treasury & Banking • Manage banking relationships and ensure smooth operations for payments and collections. • Oversee foreign exchange transactions and compliance with FEMA regulations. • Implement efficient cash management strategies. 6. Risk Management • Manage Credit Risks • Identify financial risks and implement mitigation strategies. • Ensure compliance with corporate risk management policies. 7. Stakeholder Management • Act as the primary finance contact for corporate headquarters and local authorities. • Collaborate with HR, procurement, and operations teams for financial support and guidance. 8. Further aspects • Represent the Finance, Controlling and Tax function on the company’s Steering Team. • Liaise with relevant functional counterparts in our central Controlling & Finance organization in Germany as well as in our other GCCs. • Drive improvements for processes relevant to the Finance, Controlling and Tax function. Qualifications: `• Education: Chartered Accountant (CA). • Experience: Minimum 6–8 years in finance, controlling, and taxation roles, preferably in multinational or GCC environments. • Strong knowledge of Indian GAAP, IFRS, and corporate reporting standards. • Expertise in direct Internal and indirect taxation, including GST and transfer pricing. • Hands-on experience with ERP systems (SAP preferred) and digital finance tools. • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills. • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities. • Strategic thinking with attention to detail. • Strong ethical standards and integrity. • Ability to adapt to a fast-paced, digital-first environment. • Proactive and self-motivated with leadership qualities. We are looking for experienced and motivated Controlling Professionals to join our team and play a key role in supporting our delivery organization from a finance and compliance perspective. This position is ideal for self-driven individuals who can independently manage end-to-end finance responsibilities and contribute to our growth story. Key Responsibilities and Main Tasks •    Conduct cost analysis, monitoring, reporting, and budgeting for your assigned business area. •    Liaise with the Finance Manager of the company. •    Provide actionable insights and recommendations to drive the future development of your business area within the BASF Group. •    Deliver reliable financial information to support management decision-making and strategic planning. •    Gain a comprehensive understanding of global accounting and charging mechanisms. •    Lead and support automation and continuous improvement initiatives within the team. •    Manage month-end and year-end closing processes, as well as forecasting and budget planning. •    Continuously improve and automate reporting to ensure transparency in cost development. •    Monitor project cost accounting and ensure accurate tracking. •    Prepare and deliver clear presentations to management. •    Coordinate management inquiries and structure complex ad-hoc analyses. •    Ensure compliance with BASF Group’s accounting and controlling guidelines. •    Conduct training sessions for stakeholders to promote smooth processes and effective knowledge transfer. Qualifications & Experience •    Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Controlling, or Accounting. •    Several years of relevant experience in business or controlling functions is preferred. •    Strong proficiency in controlling IT tools such as MS Office, SAP BW, SAP CO/CI, and PowerBI. •    Excellent analytical and numerical skills, with a strong ability to learn and work independently as well as in a team. •    Team player with a high service orientation and a proactive, continuous improvement mindset. •    Strong communication skills in English; knowledge of German is an added advantage but not mandatory. Why Join Us •    Be part of a dynamic, growing team at the forefront of digitalization in a global organization. •    Opportunity to work on impactful projects and contribute to strategic business decisions. •    Collaborative work environment with a focus on continuous learning and professional development. •    Competitive compensation and benefits tailored for the Indian market. name: GDS/A - Cyber Security Compliance and Assurance  (M/F/A)
description: Cyber Security Compliance and Assurance Manager Main Tasks: •    Ensuring Compliance with Security and Regulatory Requirements: Monitor and assess adherence to legal, regulatory, and internal requirements in information security and data protection. •    Conducting Audits and Assessments: Plan, coordinate, and execute internal and external audits as well as risk and compliance assessments to identify vulnerabilities and areas for improvement. •    Consulting and Training: Support and raise awareness among business units and employees on compliance and security topics; conduct awareness trainings. •    Risk Management: Identify, assess, and track risks related to information security and compliance; develop and implement risk mitigation measures. •    Reporting and Communication: Prepare compliance status reports and communicate results and recommendations to management and relevant stakeholders. •    Collaboration with Internal and External Partners: Work closely with IT, data protection, legal, internal audit, as well as external auditors and authorities. •    Preparation and Support of Certifications: Assist in the preparation and execution of certifications (e.g., ISO 27001, TISAX) and ensure ongoing compliance with requirements. •    Continuous Improvement: Analyze incidents, derive lessons learned, and continuously enhance compliance and security processes. Minimum Education and Qualification Requirements for the Position: •    Degree in IT, business informatics, engineering, or a comparable qualification. •    Several years of professional experience in information security, compliance, audit, or risk management. •    Knowledge of relevant standards and legal requirements (e.g., ISO 27001, GDPR, NIS2). •    Analytical thinking, strong communication skills, and assertiveness. •    Certifications such as CISA, CISM, CISSP, or ISO 27001 Lead Auditor are an advantage. Soft Skills: •    Exceptional collaboration and interpersonal skills with a proven ability to foster cooperation and empower a diverse team. •    Strong strategic thinking and problem-solving capabilities. •    Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, facilitating effective collaboration with diverse stakeholder groups at all levels. Tools and Technology Skills: •    Proficient in GRC tools for managing governance, risk, and compliance processes, ensuring effective integration and reporting. •    Familiarity with security frameworks and compliance standards such as ISO 27001, NIS2, and the Cyber Resilience Act, etc., to ensure proper alignment with regulatory requirements. •    Proficiency in utilizing collaboration tools such as M365 and SharePoint to streamline communication and documentation within teams.

name: GDE/ED - SAP Consultant HR Time Management
description:
GDE/ED - SAP Consultant HR Time Management Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GDE/ED - SAP Consultant HR Time Management LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now About the Role We are seeking a highly skilled SAP S/4HANA Time Management Expert who combines strong technical know how with deep functional HR Time Management expertise. In this hybrid role, you will act as a trusted advisor to our HR Business Partners and support our global Time Management processes end to end—from requirements analysis and solution design to technical implementation and operational excellence. Your mission is to ensure stable system operations, deliver high-quality solutions, and proactively guide the business in shaping efficient, compliant, and user centered time processes in an S/4HANA environment. ________________________________________ Your Responsibilities Functional Expertise & Business Consulting •    Advise HR business partners on time evaluation logic, working time models, absence processes, time accounts, and country specific regulatory requirements. •    Translate complex business requirements into robust functional concepts and guide stakeholders in process optimization. •    Conduct fit gap analyses and provide recommendations for process harmonization and simplification across regions. •    Ensure legal and policy compliance related to working time, accruals, absences, and data retention. Technical Solution Ownership •    Design, configure, and maintain SAP S/4HANA Time Management (PT), including schemas, rules, time valuation, work schedules, and master data dependencies. •    Implement technical changes and coordinate development activities with external and internal teams. •    Evaluate, test, and deploy SAP Notes, transport requests, and configuration adjustments. •    Oversee system integrations (Payroll, PA/OM, BTP, external workforce onboarding, interfaces). Operational Excellence & DevOps •    Manage day to day application operations, troubleshoot issues, and ensure system stability. •    Conduct thorough testing, regression analysis, and quality assurance for changes, upgrades, and releases. •    Drive continuous improvement initiatives, such as data cleanup, process automation, and S/4HANA simplification adoption. •    Prepare documentation, guidelines, and training materials for users and stakeholders. name: GDD/GD - Yard Management - SAP TM Functional Consultant (M/F/D)
description:
GDD/GD - Yard Management - SAP TM Functional Consultant (M/F/D) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GDD/GD - Yard Management - SAP TM Functional Consultant (M/F/D) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Yard Management – SAP TM Functional Consultant Role main activities. What is expected from you? We are looking for a seasoned SAP Transportation Management (TM) Functional Consultant with deep expertise in S/4HANA TM, a solid understanding of end-to-end supply chain logistics, and proven delivery experience in the chemical industry. The successful candidate will lead the design, configuration, drive cross-functional alignment, act as a bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams and optimise our Yard Management solutions which have a deep integration with TM. A strong background in SAP R/3 to S/4HANA migration projects is required. Functional knowledge •    Strong understanding of end-to-end transportation and logistics processes: freight order management, carrier selection, route planning, yard management, and freight settlement, milestone tracking, gate management, and inbound/outbound logistics handoffs. •    Proficiency in freight order lifecycle management, subcontracting, execution monitoring, and event management (milestones, status updates). •    Solid knowledge of SAP modules interfacing with TM: MM (Materials Management), EWM (Extended Warehouse Management), SD (Sales & Distribution), and FI/CO. •    Ability to translate complex business requirements into scalable and maintainable technical solutions. •    Ability to drive and facilitate cross-team technical discussions, aligning stakeholders from TM, EWM, Manufacturing, Core Systems, and business teams towards a common solution design. •    Understanding of multi-ERP integration scenarios •    Familiarity with BASF-relevant logistics processes in the chemical industry: bulk transport, hazardous materials, inbound/outbound logistics •    Experience supporting User Acceptance Testing (UAT), functional testing, and hypercare phases post go-live. •    Ability to drive standardisation and reduce non-standard developments by leveraging SAP standard functionalities. •    Apply a global mindset with a strong focus on reusability, standardisation, and harmonisation when designing and developing new solutions – ensuring scalability across BASF’s global landscape. Technical Knowledge •    Strong understanding of ABAP development, including object-oriented ABAP (OO-ABAP). •    Deep knowledge of API/event-based architecture: Kafka, SAP API Management, SAP Integration Suite (CPI), and event-driven design patterns for real-time logistics data flows •    Familiarity in SAP S/4HANA migration tools and methodologies (SUM, SPDD/SPAU, custom code adaptation, Simplification Item checks). •    Familiarity with SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) Relevant Professional experience Overall, we are looking for an SAP TM Functional Consultant with +4 years of IT industry experience in SAP software implementation with increasing levels of responsibility. We require: •    Proven track record in at least one full-cycle SAP TM implementation or major upgrade project. •    Demonstrated experience in SAP R/3 to S/4HANA migration projects •    Experience working in global, multicultural, and geographically distributed teams, ideally in a shared services or digital hub environment. •    Background in the chemical, manufacturing, or process industry is a strong advantage, with exposure to multi-site logistics models. •    Experience delivering IT solutions in an agile product model, including sprint planning, backlog refinement, and DevOps practices. •    Hands-on involvement in system integration projects with third-party logistics providers (3PLs), carriers, or external platforms. •    Proven experience in driving and coordinating System Integration Testing (SIT) across multiple workstreams, including test planning, defect management, and sign-off in complex multi-system landscapes. •    Experience with application lifecycle management, change/release management, and quality assurance processes. •    Experience running IT solutions as products (product ownership mindset) and contributing to continuous improvement initiatives. Relevant Education •    Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, or a related field from an accredited university. •    Advanced English skills – written and verbal; additional European languages are a plus. •    Growth mindset with a genuine curiosity to learn, adapt, and improve in a fast-evolving technology landscape. •    Strong team player with excellent interpersonal, communication, and stakeholder management skills. •    Ability to work efficiently under pressure on non-routine and highly complex tasks. •    Comfortable working in a multicultural environment across multiple time zones. Nice to Have •    Experience with additional programming languages such as Java, Python, or JavaScript. •    Knowledge of Jira and/or Microsoft Azure DevOps for project and backlog management. •    SAP certification in TM, S/4HANA Logistics, or related areas. •    Experience with SAP Yard Logistics (YL) as a complement to TM. •    Familiarity with core lean practices and IT product management methodologies. • Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. name: GDB/ZI - Integration Platform Engineer (M/F/D)
description:
GDB/ZI - Integration Platform Engineer (M/F/D) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GDB/ZI - Integration Platform Engineer (M/F/D) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Objectives Describe your Product mission here (2 - 3 sentences only) Join our “Data Foundation” team and work on the cutting-edge “Integration” product that leverages innovative technologies to seamlessly connect applications and facilitate data exchange. The Integration Platform is a versatile framework for building and executing integration scenarios—serving both internal and external needs. With the platform and its underlying technologies, you’ll have access to advanced tools and processes designed to connect to any type of data, in any format you require. As part of our mission, you’ll help implement Enterprise Integration Concept capabilities across Data Integration, Thing Integration, Process Integration, and User Integration. If you’re passionate about driving digital transformation and want to make a real impact in Hyderabad, this is your opportunity to be at the forefront of integration technology. Describe the objectives of the role here (2 - 3 sentences only) Be part of building the future of integration platform in Hyderabad! As an Integration Platform Engineer, you will play a key role in shaping a self-service, governed, and highly visible integration lifecycle for APIs, events, and workflows. Your expertise will help design and develop essential backend services that drive orchestration, governance, discovery, and automation—leveraging the power of Kubernetes. Join us to create robust solutions that accelerate digital transformation and make a real impact on global business operations. Main Tasks / Scope of Work Our main requirements: §    Create lifecycle workflows with Argo (or Camunda) and connect them to backend services and Kafka events. §    Include governance and approval steps, and guarantee that processes are auditable and traceable. §    Develop and deliver platform services using Spring Boot, ensuring robust and scalable solutions for our Hyderabad team. §    Create and maintain REST APIs to support registry, marketplace backend, lifecycle management, and governance processes. §    Integrate and manage PostgreSQL databases, optimising performance and reliability for business-critical applications. §    Implement automation workflows utilising Argo or Camunda to streamline and enhance operational efficiency. §    Produce and consume Kafka events, enabling seamless communication and data exchange across platform components. Mandatory Personal traits §    Demonstrates an entrepreneurial mindset, embracing innovation and new opportunities §    Shows a strong sense of accountability and ownership in all tasks §    Adopts a proactive approach, taking initiative to drive results §    Possesses high levels of ambition and a strong commitment to delivering excellent service §    Exhibits effective communication and strong interpersonal skills §    Capable of managing and resolving conflicts, with an awareness of potential issues §    Maintains a strong focus on customer satisfaction at all times §    Works collaboratively and contributes positively within a team environment §    Comfortable working as part of a DevOps team in a dynamic setting §    Demonstrates adaptability and is able to respond effectively to unexpected challenges Nice to have: §    Expertise in multiple integration technologies, such as SAP Integration Suite, SAP API Management, Kafka, or comparable platforms §    Understanding of data management and governance principles and best practices §    Proficient in database administration, ensuring optimum performance and reliability Job Requirements Focus on the minimum requirements to carry out the work – you might want to point out “additional plus/nice to have skills” that are not mandatory Education and work experience    §    Degree in computer science, information technology, business informatics, or a related discipline §    At least 3-5 years of relevant professional experience §    Familiarity with Agile methodologies or experience in Agile implementation §    Collaborative mindset, with the ability to work effectively in cross-functional, multicultural DevOps teams. Experience in large-scale enterprise environments is highly desirable. Technical & Professional Knowledge Mandatory expertise in •    Experience with setting up and operating Argo Workflows (or Camunda) in Kubernetes (k8s) •    Integration of systems using REST APIs •    Kubernetes (application operation) §    Knative §    Knowledge of Kafka •    Traefik Ingress •    Securing Microservices •    Azure Key Vault §    cert-manager Nice to have •    Familiarity with BPMN Expertise in Software Development •    Programming Languages and Frameworks: Extensive hands-on experience in Java and Spring Boot, with a robust understanding of building scalable and maintainable backend systems. •    API Design and Integration: Demonstrated ability to design, develop, and maintain RESTful APIs, ensuring secure and efficient data exchange between services and client applications. •    Database Management: Proficient in PostgreSQL, including schema design, query optimisation, and integration with enterprise applications. •    Containerisation and Orchestration: Strong working knowledge of Kubernetes at the application level, including deployment, scaling, and management of containerised microservices. Practical experience with Knative for managing serverless workloads on Kubernetes clusters. •    Streaming and Messaging: Hands-on expertise with Kafka at the application level, enabling reliable data streaming and event-driven architectures within distributed environments. •    Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment: Track record of designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines to automate testing, integration, and deployment processes, ensuring rapid and reliable software delivery. description:

Conducting Audits and Assessments: Plan, coordinate, and execute internal and external audits as well as risk and compliance assessments to identify vulnerabilities and areas for improvement. Consulting and Training: Support and raise awareness among business units and employees on compliance and security topics; conduct awareness trainings. Risk Management: Identify, assess, and track risks related to information security and compliance; develop and implement risk mitigation measures. Reporting and Communication: Prepare compliance status reports and communicate results and recommendations to management and relevant stakeholders. Preparation and Support of Certifications: Assist in the preparation and execution of certifications (e.g., ISO 27001, TISAX) and ensure ongoing compliance with requirements. Continuous Improvement: Analyze incidents, derive lessons learned, and continuously enhance compliance and security processes. Qualification Comprehensive understanding of regulatory requirements and industry standards related to Cyber Security risk management and compliance. Solid foundation in Cyber Security control frameworks based on ISO 27001, enabling the identification and implementation of mitigating measures for strategic risks while effectively balancing risk severity with business opportunities and operational impact across business units. Proficient in GRC tools for managing governance, risk, and compliance processes, ensuring effective integration and reporting. Familiarity with security frameworks and compliance standards such as ISO 27001, NIS2, and the Cyber Resilience Act to ensure proper alignment with regulatory requirements. Proficiency in collaboration tools such as M365 and SharePoint to streamline communication and documentation within teams. Senior Business Solution Consultant - SAP QM (Quality Management) (m/f/d) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Responsibilities: - You will act as the global Business Solution Consultant for SAP QM within an R3 and S/4HANA landscape, ensuring scalable, standardized, and future-ready quality management solutions. - You will design, implement, and continuously improve end-to-end Quality Management processes (Quality Planning, Quality Inspection, Quality Notifications, Audit Management, Stability Studies, Certificates of Analysis). - You will support the transition and optimization from SAP ECC/R3 to S/4HANA, leveraging standard functionalities, Fiori apps, embedded analytics, and best-practice content. - You will collaborate closely with business stakeholders (Quality Assurance, Quality Control, Supply Chain, Manufacturing, Regulatory) to translate business requirements into sustainable system solutions. - You will ensure integration of SAP QM with adjacent modules and platforms (MM, PP, EWM, SD, PM, IBP, PLM), as well as external systems such as Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS), lab instruments, MES, and digital quality platforms. - You will support digitalization initiatives, including automation of Certificates of Analysis, integration of analytical devices, and enablement of advanced analytics, AI/ML use cases, and predictive quality scenarios. - You will contribute to global template governance, defining guardrails and ensuring adherence to architectural and security standards. - You will participate in project phases (fit-to-standard workshops, blueprint/design, realization, testing, cutover, hypercare, and continuous improvement). - You will mentor junior consultants and support knowledge transfer to ensure long-term sustainability of the solution landscape. Qualifications: - You have hands-on experience with SAP QM in S/4HANA (including configuration and functional design). Experience with S/4HANA migration or transformation programs is required. - You possess in-depth knowledge of Quality Management processes within manufacturing and supply chain environments. - You have solid understanding of system integration concepts (IDocs, APIs, OData services, middleware platforms such as SAP CPI/PI/PO or similar). - You are experienced in working within global template-driven environments and understand the balance between standardization and local requirements. - You have knowledge of data modeling, master data governance, and embedded analytics in S/4HANA. - Exposure to digital lab integration, automation of quality data capture, and/or LIMS integration is highly desirable. - Familiarity with emerging technologies such as AI/ML, RPA, or advanced analytics in quality scenarios is considered an advantage. Internal - You are familiar with agile delivery methodologies (Scrum, SAFe, DevOps mindset) and tools such as Azure DevOps, or similar. - You demonstrate strong stakeholder management skills and can effectively operate in a global, cross-functional environment. - You have excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills in English Profession / Occupation / Studies: - Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Chemistry, or related field. - Alternatively, equivalent professional experience in SAP Quality Management within manufacturing or chemical/pharmaceutical industries. In this role, you will be responsible for central Windows workplace applications such as Adobe Acrobat, SAP GUI, and similar tools that are used by BASF employees worldwide. You will work in an international team and act as a confident interface between internal teams, business partners, and external service providers. You will take ownership of complex application landscapes and ensure stable, efficient, and future ready services across BASF. Your Key Responsibilities •    Application Ownership & Support o    Advise and support BASF employees in the use of Windows workplace applications. o    Analyze major incidents and recurring issues and implement sustainable, long term solutions within the IT landscape. •    Collaboration & Stakeholder Management o    Work closely with internal departments such as Procurement and Global License Management. o    Provide expert input and actively contribute to, or lead, cross functional and international projects. •    Service Provider Management o    Coordinate and steer external service providers. o    Ensure high service quality, continuous improvement, and professional complaint management. •    Innovation & Continuous Improvement o    Drive the development and introduction of new solutions and technologies together with external partners. o    Identify improvement potential and proactively contribute to the digital workplace strategy. •    Service & Process Excellence o    Design, implement, and continuously optimize support and service processes. o    Monitor service performance based on agreed Service Level Agreements (SLAs). o    Prepare reports and initiate improvement measures to ensure high user satisfaction. name: GDE/AC - Senior Automation Engineer (m/f/d)
description:
GDE/AC - Senior Automation Engineer (m/f/d) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GDE/AC - Senior Automation Engineer (m/f/d) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now About the Role We are hiring a Senior Automation Engineer to help transform how we run and support our SAP application landscape.  Your mission is simple but ambitious: eliminate manual work, reduce operational friction, and build smart automation that scales. You will work hands ‑ on on automating Application Management Support (AMS) tasks as well as for the SAP operations of our major SAP Landscape (release management, work request handling, authorizations, regression testing, etc) using a modern enterprise technology stack. SAP (S/4HANA, Cloud ALM, SAP AI/Joule) is a core element—but success in this role depends on your ability to integrate and automate across SAP, Microsoft, ServiceNow, and other automation tools. This role is central to shaping the next generation of autonomous and intelligent support processes, leveraging the latest capabilities in SAP, agentic automation, and other capabilities. ### What You Will Work On Automate operational processes across: Incident, service request, and work intake handling Support triage, enrichment, and resolution steps Knowledge ‑ supported troubleshooting and guided resolution Reduce repetitive manual effort in AMS and improve speed, stability, and quality. Release & Testing Automation Release coordination and validation Deployment and change checks Regression testing execution, reporting, and test data handling Improve release reliability while reducing operational overhead. #### Automation Engineering Design and implement automation using a mixed technology ecosystem: SAP (S/4HANA, Cloud ALM, SAP AI/Joule, BTP services where relevant) Microsoft (Azure services, Power Automate, Copilot, scripting) ServiceNow (workflows, orchestration, integrations) Other fit ‑ for ‑ purpose automation, orchestration, or testing tools. Work with emerging capabilities such as SAP Joule and Microsoft Copilot without being limited to them. #### Engineering Collaboration Work closely with AMS teams, release managers, testers, and architects. Design and develop an agent-based automation framework to support Application Management processes for the future SAP S/4 landscape. Identify, design, and implement automation use cases across Application Management Support, reducing manual workload and improving service quality. Contribute to shared standards, patterns, and reusable building blocks. Define standards, reusable components, and best practices for agentic automation. Drive continuous improvement and scalability of the automation framework. Ensure compliance with corporate IT, security, and data governance guidelines. ### What We Are Looking For Strong hands ‑ on experience in application operations, AMS, or enterprise IT environments Strong experience with SAP S/4HANA, especially in Application Management or Support-related processes. Hands-on experience with SAP Joule and Joule Studio (designing, building, and maintaining Joule Skills). Deep understanding of agentic frameworks, AI-driven workflow automation, or LLM based Assistants. Ability to translate functional support needs into scalable automated solutions. Solid knowledge of enterprise integrations (APIs, events, orchestration). Experience with SAP S/4HANA operations or support tooling (SAP BTP, SAP Integration Suite, Cloud ALM, SolMan, SAP testing tools), ServiceNow capabilities for support automation, workflows, orchestration and other tools supporting automation Proven experience building automation beyond simple scripting, across workflows or systems. Solid understanding of IT service processes (incident, change, release, testing). Understanding of ITIL processes and Application Management methodologies. Exposure to AI ‑ driven automation, copilots, or agent ‑ based approaches. Experience with **test automation or release pipelines in enterprise landscapes. Awareness of security, access, and compliance considerations in automation. Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a

description:

We are looking for experienced and motivated Controlling Professionals to join our team and play a key role in supporting our delivery organization from a finance and compliance perspective. This position is ideal for self-driven individuals who can independently manage end-to-end finance responsibilities and contribute to our growth story. Key Responsibilities and Main Tasks Conduct cost analysis, monitoring, reporting, and budgeting for your assigned business area. Liaise with the Finance Manager of the company. Provide actionable insights and recommendations to drive the future development of your business area within the BASF Group. Deliver reliable financial information to support management decision-making and strategic planning. Gain a comprehensive understanding of global accounting and charging mechanisms. Lead and support automation and continuous improvement initiatives within the team. Manage month-end and year-end closing processes, as well as forecasting and budget planning. Continuously improve and automate reporting to ensure transparency in cost development. Monitor project cost accounting and ensure accurate tracking. Prepare and deliver clear presentations to management. Coordinate management inquiries and structure complex ad-hoc analyses. Ensure compliance with BASF Group’s accounting and controlling guidelines. Conduct training sessions for stakeholders to promote smooth processes and effective knowledge transfer. Qualifications & Experience Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business , Finance, Controlling, or Accounting. Several years of relevant experience in business or controlling functions is preferred. Strong proficiency in controlling IT tools such as MS Office, SAP BW, SAP CO/CI, and PowerBI . Excellent analytical and numerical skills, with a strong ability to learn and work independently as well as in a team. Team player with a high service orientation and a proactive, continuous improvement mindset. Strong communication skills in English; knowledge of German is an added advantage but not mandatory. Why Join Us Be part of a dynamic, growing team at the forefront of digitalization in a global organization. Opportunity to work on impactful projects and contribute to strategic business decisions. Collaborative work environment with a focus on continuous learning and professional development. Competitive compensation and benefits tailored for the Indian market. Responsibilities: We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Full-Stack Engineer to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will be responsible for developing and maintaining the infrastructure components. You will work closely with our senior developers to create high quality, scalable, and responsive software solutions.  Template Development: Design and build reusable templates and components for web teams to streamline their development processes.  - User Experience Enhancement: Enhance the user experience on the platform by developing intuitive and responsive front-end interfaces.  - CI/CD Pipelines: Collaborate with the DevOps team to develop, run, and automate CI/CD pipelines for front-end and full stack applications.  - Compliance and Security: Ensure adherence to BASF compliance and security guidelines in web development.  Process Improvement: Contribute to the improvement of internal development processes. Qualifications: - Front-End Technologies: Proficiency in front-end technologies such as HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and frameworks like React, Angular, or Vue.js. - Back-End Technologies: Experience with back-end technologies such as Node.js, Python, or Java. Ability to develop and integrate RESTful APIs and microservices. - Cloud Expertise: Demonstrated experience with cloud platforms, particularly Azure. Ability to design, implement, and manage cloud infrastructure for full-stack applications. - Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Experience with Terraform for provisioning and managing infrastructure. Ability to write and maintain modular, reusable, and scalable Terraform code. - CI/CD Pipelines: Experience in setting up and maintaining CI/CD pipelines for full-stack applications. Familiarity with GitHub Actions or similar tools is highly desirable. - Kubernetes: Experience with deploying, scaling, and managing full-stack applications in Kubernetes environments. - Agile Methodologies: Proven experience working in Agile environments. Strong understanding of Agile principles and practices, including Scrum or Kanban. Ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams. - Product Thinking: Ability to approach problems with a product mindset, focusing on delivering value to end-users. Experience in iterative development and continuous improvement based on user feedback. - Collaboration and Communication: Excellent interpersonal and communication skills. Ability to work collaboratively with team members, stakeholders, and clients to achieve project goals. - Problem-Solving Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities. Capacity to troubleshoot complex technical issues and provide innovative solutions. - Continuous Learning: Commitment to staying updated with the latest industry trends, technologies, and best practices. Eagerness to continuously improve skills and knowledge. - Certifications: Relevant certifications in front end frameworks, Azure, Terraform, Kubernetes, or Agile methodologies are a plus. - Proficiency in GitHub for version control, collaboration, and CI/CD workflows is highly preferred. - Experience with GitHub Actions for automating CI/CD processes.  - Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, or a related field. - Minimum of 6 years of experience as a Full-Stack Engineer or similar role. - Experience in software Internal development methodologies such as Kanban or Scrum. - Experience in working in cross functional teams. Drive continuous improvement of S/4 operational processes— leveraging AI where beneficial—ensuring harmonization, lean practices, and structured product lifecycle management with a strong focus on radical standardization. Develop internal expertise to ensure long-term operational excellence across the S/4 solution landscape. Contribute to the organizational development of our broader team and be an active member of a forward ‑ looking leadership team. While we have a focus on SAP Authorization, we are very much welcoming candidate with a broader SAP experience, mainly with Test & release management , deployment & SAP development management and cross SAP services . Here are some of your key responsibilities, which can be applied to SAP cross topics beyond authorizations: Global SAP Authorization Ownership: Leads SAP authorization implementation and support including delivery of SAP Authorizations for projects across BASF and serves as the primary contact for audits assessments based on Corporate Guidelines. You drive innovation, automation and leverage AI capabilities in day-to-day operations. Leadership of a Distributed Global Team: Leads a worldwide SAP Authorization product team and steers external global service providers to ensure efficient, high quality service delivery. Owns budgeting, resource planning, team development, goal setting, and performance management aligned with corporate standards and strategic transformation goals. End to End DevOps Accountability for SAP Authorizations: Drives all DevOps activities based on agile methodologies for continuous operational improvement of SAP Authorization concepts and the related implementations. Qualifications Completed university degree in Business Informatics, Business Administration or Computer Science with several years of professional experience Several years of professional experience in various areas in the IT/digital environment, including SAP, as well as experience in application projects Practiced leadership skills (orchestration and management of cross-functional topics), entrepreneurial thinking and organizational awareness Ability to critically question the status quo and openness for new trends and innovations Strong communication skills, effective communication and very good written and spoken English skills Good conflict management skills, high customer orientation and empathy when dealing with stakeholders across the organization Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our dedicated teams deliver tailored digital products and services to internal business partners worldwide, leveraging the power of our Digital Hubs as dynamic centers of innovation and collaboration that bring us closer to our customers and regional needs. Here, you’ll join a high-performance culture inspired by our Winning Ways: enabling our customers’ green transformation, accelerating sustainability, and creating a workplace for curious, ambitious talents. In the Core Systems (ERP) & Foundation Product Family, we provide a stable yet innovative platform for BASF’s global ERP (SAP) environment, foster collaboration across our global community and establish guiding principles that drive digitalization. Together, we shape BASF’s digital future. Join our SAP Authorizations Product Team and be part of a global community where your ideas and expertise make a real impact. Responsibilities •    Design, implement, and operate compliant SAP authorization roles and user access to support BASF’s digital transformation •    Ensure secure and compliant platform operations through accurate authorization design and issue resolution •    Govern and steer authorization delivery by suppliers, including request fulfillment, escalation management, and SLA compliance •    Develop, enhance, and maintain SAP authorization concepts, guidelines, and security requirements •    Monitor existing authorization setups, define critical access objects, and perform authorization clean ups based on security assessments •    Optimize efficiency and performance through cost effective solutions, continuous improvement, and use of AI enabled capabilities •    Apply certified SAP add on products to ensure a compliant and robust authorization environment •    Independently manage tasks across multiple interfaces with strong international and cross organizational coordination •    Act as Lead Consultant for SAP Authorizations in projects, influencing solution design and delivery •    Communicate and negotiate complex or sensitive topics with diverse stakeholders, building commitment and alignment •    Contribute as a team player by sharing knowledge, best practices, and supporting collaborative problem solving •    Identify and drive innovative solutions by taking a holistic perspective in a complex work environment •    Resolve topics with a focus on operational excellence, balancing efficiency, risk, and implementation complexity •    Contribute significantly to customer, operational, project, and service objectives within the product scope •    Ensure stable and secure operation of BASF’s global SAP landscape, including on call support when required •    Support internal and external audits through analysis, reporting, and continuous quality improvements Requirements •    Completed university degree in Business IT, Business Economics, Information Science, Engineering, or Natural Science, with at least 4 years of professional experience in SAP security and SAP authorization  in both operations and projects •    Very good conflict ability and awareness •    Very good command of written and spoken English, in addition to the respective native language •    Strong technical expertise in SAP authorization and user management, basic platform programming languages & debugging •    Experience in supplier management and steering, agile ways of working (Agile Methodology, Growth Mindset, Continuous Learning, Customer Focus, User Stories, Metrics, Collaborative Development, Azure DevOps) •    Solid knowledge in service management processes and ServiceNow platform usage •    Excellent problem-solving skills (creativity, detail orientation) •    Strong self-management (time management, adaptability) •    Ability to manage additional responsibilities outside of own scope and to communicate/present complex topics to diverse audiences. •    Excellent knowledge in setting up and managing SAP authorizations for SAP R/3 and S/4 systems as well as BTP, BDC, HANA and Fiori, including solid SAP development knowledge What We Offer •    Being part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. •    Shape the future of offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. •    Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. • Working agile, we enable functionality of core systems & processes.  We partner and consult with stakeholders to innovate solutions proactively across multiple technology platforms to anticipate future needs. This position is especially foreseen to support the migration from an R/3 ERP to S/4 HANA and a Multi-ERP Approach of BASF. Your main responsibilities include: Support gathering of requirements and creation of user stories. Design solutions and do configurations/customization. Technical design of application enhancements and new applications for BASF Order to Cash processes. Development of those applications by using state-of-the-art programming methods and ensuring compliance with all relevant BASF guidelines for development (clean core, cloud compliance, etc.) Management of complex development environments using both internal and external resources Ensures runnable, supportable and stable solutions from a technical point of view together with operations. Contributes to planning and provides cost estimations for complex projects. Supports creation of (functional) Solution Design Documents Supports testing and error correction activities. Support the technical consultants in functional topics. Prepare and execute functional tests. Maintain user documentation. Collaborate with internal BASF partners. Work on tickets that cannot be solved by the first level support. This comprises analysis of the issues, implementation of solutions, instruction, and training of the affected user. Support change requests / projects with effort estimations. Active and proactive collaborating, self-organized pulling of tasks from a backlog. Support of the BASF Digital roadmap, helping GDS to identify, execute and implement customer-centric digital solutions in an actionable and measurable way. Our main requirements: Education Successfully completed studies in computer science, business informatics or a comparable qualification Required skills / experience: Minimum 4-6 years related working experience with complex SAP applications. Hands ‑ on experience in SAP Settlement Management / CCM, with strong knowledge of rebate and commission processes, condition contracts, accruals, settlement flows, and FI integration Experience with S/4HANA, supported by functional knowledge of SAP SD Order-to-Cash processes, including sales orders, invoicing, pricing conditions, and related SD configuration/customizing. Strong understanding of SAP pricing procedures, condition types, and access sequences Outputs (Adobe Forms, Smart Form) (incl. Qualifications: - Engineering Mastery: You are a software engineer first. You write clean, testable, production-grade Python (FastAPI, Pydantic) and are comfortable with async/await patterns and concurrency. - Container Native: You don't just run Docker locally; you have experience deploying and debugging complex applications on Kubernetes (AKS). - Agentic Frameworks: Deep, hands-on experience with LangChain, LangGraph, or Semantic Kernel. You know how to debug a "looping" agent and how to manage conversation memory effectively. - Azure & Databricks: Practical experience with Azure AI Foundry for model serving and Databricks for data engineering. - Problem Solver: You enjoy debugging non deterministic systems and finding creative ways to make LLMs reliable (e.g., retry logic, output parsing, verification steps). What we offer: Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence.  [to include specific local benefits]. name: GDB/X - Agentic AI Architect (m/f/d)
description:
GDB/X - Agentic AI Architect (m/f/d) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GDB/X - Agentic AI Architect (m/f/d) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Agentic AI Architect OrgCode: GDB/X Product Team: Intelligent Automation Responsibilities: - Architect the Agent Backbone: Define and maintain the enterprise reference architecture for autonomous agents, ensuring seamless interoperability between managed services (Azure AI Foundry) and custom open-source runtimes on AKS. - Define Agentic Standards: Own the technical standards for the ecosystem, including the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for tool interoperability and the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) communication protocol. - Design the Orchestration Layer: Lead the architectural design of our central semantic router hosted on AKS, that interprets user intent and orchestrates workflows across distributed, cross-platform agents. - Enable Hybrid Innovation: Create "Golden Path" blueprints that leverage Databricks (Unity Catalog) for massive-scale data context while utilizing AKS to host complex, stateful agent swarms using frameworks like LangGraph or AutoGen. - Drive Co-Creation & Governance: Partner with other platform teams (SAP, Salesforce) to co-architect complex cross-domain workflows (Tier 3-4) and serve as the primary technical authority for validating high-risk agent deployments. - Ensure Zero-Trust Security: Embed security-by-design principles into our agent frameworks, ensuring every agent action is authenticated, authorized, and auditable within our Guardrail Engine. Qualifications: - Agentic Mastery: You possess deep conceptual and practical knowledge of agent design patterns (ReAct, Plan-and-Solve, Swarms, Hierarchical Teams) and know when to use each. - Hybrid Infrastructure Expert: You are fluent in Azure AI Foundry for governance and model management, but you are equally comfortable architecting custom solutions on AKS to run open-source frameworks (LangChain/LangGraph) when managed services fall short. - Data-Driven Architecture: Strong experience with Databricks, understanding how to expose complex data pipelines as "Tools" for agents and how to manage agent memory/state at scale. - API & Protocol Design: Proven ability to design robust, event driven architectures (CloudEvents, Kafka) and standard APIs (FastAPI, GraphQL, MCP) for distributed systems. - Strategic Leadership: You can communicate complex architectural decisions to stakeholders and mentor senior engineers, fostering a culture of technical excellence. - Tech Stack You Will Master: -- Orchestration: LangGraph, AutoGen, Semantic Kernel, Azure AI Foundry. -- Compute & Runtime: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), Docker, Azure Functions. -- Data & Memory: Databricks (Unity Catalog), Vector Databases (Qdrant/Pinecone), Redis. -- Languages: Python (Expert), TypeScript (Proficient). -- Standards: Model Context Protocol (MCP), OpenTelemetry. description:
GDD/EN - Solution designer 3rd party SW solutions (m/f/d) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GDD/EN - Solution designer 3rd party SW solutions (m/f/d) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Summary Digitalization will be a true part of BASF’s DNA that creates new exciting customer experiences and business growth as well as drives efficiencies in processes. Global Digital Services is supporting BASF business in Digitalization transformation. Our mission is to drive forward the digital transformation of BASF, providing innovative, global and high-quality digital products and services through a strong agile culture. The Digital Hub Hyderabad is one of our global key locations to deliver regional and global solutions for BASF. Responsibilities and Key Outcomes Our team supports the implementation, maintenance, and operation of digital solutions for BASF. This includes the planning and support of complex Data & IT landscapes incl. infrastructure for the client-/server and Web- applications, packaging and rollout of applications, and integration of cloud solutions. We coordinate the relevant activities with the respective providers as well as with BASF’s internal IT units (e.g., security, data integration, infrastructure/cloud enablement, system integration). Furthermore, we develop interfaces for a seamless integration of the different systems into the whole IT landscape. We support the solutions using BASF's IT Service Management processes such as incident & change management and accompany our customers during the entire solution lifecycle. You are responsible for continuous service delivery respective IT landscape: incident management, change management, updates, upgrades, modernization, migration, security checks, lifecycle management. Hereby you interact with different product teams. Your main responsibility will be the integration and lifecycle support of 3rd party Software (Commercial of the shelf SW) into the BASFs infrastructure environment. Therefore, a wide infrastructure and integration knowledge is essential. Job Requirements Education and work experience    •    Master / bachelor’s degree in information technologies, comparable qualification, or many years of experience in the relevant work area •    Minimum of 5 years related working experience TECHNICAL EXPERTISE •    Experience in heterogeneous enterprise IT environments (Windows, Linux, Networks) •    Technical security & IAM understanding according to corporate standards •    Third-party software integration (design, infrastructure, security, connectivity) •    Lifecycle management, updates, end of life (EoL) •    At least 4 years of service experience with Microsoft SQL servers (DML & DDL) •    Basic knowledge of PostgreSQL databases •    At least 4 years of service experience with Oracle databases (DML & DDL) (including the latest versions) •    Basic knowledge of webserver installation & configuration (Tomcat, Apache, IIS) •    At least 4 years of service experience of Windows, Linux and/or Unix based operating systems (knowledge equivalent to level LPIC-1 for Linux or Windows Server Administration Fundamentals) •    At least 4 years of service experience for software installations •    At least 2 years of service experience for webservices (JSON, SOAP, REST API including OpenAPI V3.0 development) – No API Development, only integration!!! •    At least 2 years of service experience with complex system landscapes (different servers, networks, security zones, interfaces) •    Basic knowledge with common cloud concepts (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS, Docker, Kubernetes) (level of Microsoft Azure Fundamentals) •    At least 2 years of service experience of docker technologies: Application container, Orchestration, Container-anatomy, Request-routing with Traefik, Continuous Integration und DevOps, Output and logging •    At least 2 years of service experience in certificate handling (ACME) •    Basic knowledge of ITIL processes and CMDB (ServiceNow) •    Experience with Change-, Release  und Incident Management •    Technical understanding of security and IAM according to corporate standards Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. The role bridges architecture, engineering, and enablement — ensuring both reliable technical operations and broad business adoption across domains. Design, implement, and maintain the overall SAP Datasphere platform architecture. Establish data domain-driven and data product-based design principles.  Integrate SAP (S/4HANA, BW/4HANA) and non-SAP data sources (Azure, APIs) via ODP, SDI, or native connectors  Build and optimize data pipelines, semantic models, and analytical datasets. Ensure robust data governance, access control, and security configuration.  Lead platform evolution towards a federated, business data fabric model.  Collaborate with enterprise architects, product family architects, data governance, SAP- and cloud infrastructure teams.  Develop enablement strategies, best practices, and onboarding frameworks for data domain teams.  Conduct training, create documentation, and foster community of practice for self-service use cases.  Monitor platform performance, manage incidents, and ensure system reliability.  Drive continuous improvement through automation (CI/CD, DevOps for data) and usage analytics. Qualifications: 5+ years of professional experience in data architecture, integration, or analytics platforms. Proven expertise in SAP Datasphere (or formerly SAP Data Warehouse Cloud) administration, modeling, and operations.  Strong knowledge of SAP BTP services, connectivity, and hybrid integration technologies.  Deep understanding of semantic modeling, data lineage, and data virtualization concepts.  Proficiency in SQL and experience with scripting (Python, ABAP CDS, or similar).  Hands-on experience with SAP Analytics Cloud or equivalent BI tools.  Familiarity with data governance frameworks (DAMA, Business Data Fabric).  Knowledge of cloud architectures (Azure, GCP, AWS) and federated query patterns.  Strong communication skills to collaborate across IT and business domains.  Experience in coaching, enablement, or community building within data initiatives.  Practical experience in authorisation concept design and implementation in Datasphere, ideally with 3rd party IAM tool What we offer: Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. We are looking for experienced and motivated Controlling Professionals to join our team and play a key role in supporting our delivery organization from a finance and compliance perspective. This position is ideal for self-driven individuals who can independently manage end-to-end finance responsibilities and contribute to our growth story. Key Responsibilities and Main Tasks Conduct cost analysis, monitoring, reporting, and budgeting for your assigned business area. Liaise with the Finance Manager of the company. Provide actionable insights and recommendations to drive the future development of your business area within the BASF Group. Deliver reliable financial information to support management decision-making and strategic planning. Gain a comprehensive understanding of global accounting and charging mechanisms. Lead and support automation and continuous improvement initiatives within the team. Manage month-end and year-end closing processes, as well as forecasting and budget planning. Continuously improve and automate reporting to ensure transparency in cost development. Monitor project cost accounting and ensure accurate tracking. Prepare and deliver clear presentations to management. Coordinate management inquiries and structure complex ad-hoc analyses. Ensure compliance with BASF Group’s accounting and controlling guidelines. Conduct training sessions for stakeholders to promote smooth processes and effective knowledge transfer. Qualifications & Experience Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Controlling, or Accounting. Several years of relevant experience in business or controlling functions is preferred. Strong proficiency in controlling IT tools such as MS Office, SAP BW, SAP CO/CI, and PowerBI. Excellent analytical and numerical skills, with a strong ability to learn and work independently as well as in a team. Team player with a high service orientation and a proactive, continuous improvement mindset. Strong communication skills in English; knowledge of German is an added advantage but not mandatory. Why Join Us Be part of a dynamic, growing team at the forefront of digitalization in a global organization. Opportunity to work on impactful projects and contribute to strategic business decisions. Collaborative work environment with a focus on continuous learning and professional development. Competitive compensation and benefits tailored for the Indian market. You will focus on the technical foundations that enable scalable, secure, and high-performing reporting and data-driven actionable insights solutions, with a strong emphasis on Power BI integrations, data connections, and embedding scenarios. You define and implement advanced reporting technical patterns, including dataset reuse, composite models, incremental refresh strategies, and performance optimization using DAX and model design best practices. Your responsibilities include defining and implementing connection strategies between reporting platforms and multiple data sources, optimizing data access patterns, and supporting complex integration scenarios. You play a key role in enabling advanced use cases such as embedding Power BI reports into external applications (e.g. Salesforce) and ensuring secure authentication and authorization flows. In addition, you contribute to the evolution of actionable customer insights by enabling advanced capabilities such as AI-driven analytics, Copilot features, or chatbot-based access to data, in alignment with the overall reporting strategy. You will collaborate closely with the Reporting Lead and Product Owner to translate strategic and architectural decisions into robust technical implementations for Sales reporting across BASF ODs. WHAT YOU BRING Degree in computer science, data engineering, analytics, or comparable technical qualification >5 years of experience with Salesforce (Sales/Service Cloud) as a developer and architect, solid understanding of complex data models and system architectures, and ideally Salesforce certifications (e.g., System Administrator, AI Specialist) Strong experience with data platforms, particularly Azure Databricks. Well proficient in the uses of Azure DevOps (or similar tools such as Jira) as part of SDLC. Solid understanding of data integration, authentication, and embedding scenarios, ideally via an event-driven architecture (e.g., Kafka as the event bus). Experience with data classification, using AI models (e.g., OpenAI LLMs for summarization), and generating data-driven insights. Azure OpenAI (e.g., GPT models), Prompt Flow, integration of Azure OpenAI into Databricks. GenAI patterns: RAG, vector databases, LLM agents (including interfaces such as MCP/A2A), prompt engineering and evaluation. Very strong Python skills; frameworks such as PyTorch/TensorFlow/sklearn Strong experience in Power BI architecture, advanced features and deep technical knowledge of Power BI Service, semantic models, datasets, and security (RLS, AAD) Embedding expertise web development skills are important to use APIs and create modern, user-friendly interfaces. This also includes using frameworks and libraries provided by the DevHub platform, as well as automation and CI/CD pipelines for rapid development and deployment Experience working in agile environments and with tools such as Azure DevOps and Confluence Analytical skills and enjoyment of teamwork, combined with a structured and solution-oriented working style Enthusiasm for diverse tasks that allow you to apply your strengths as a technical allrounder Very good English skills, both written and spoken Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Your Responsibilities SAP PM Service Delivery & Key Account Responsibility Act as first point of contact (Key Account) for assigned plants for all questions related to SAP PM solutions within the product Digital Asset Master Data Management . SAP PM Master Data Management Deliver SAP PM base services, including: Creation and maintenance of Functional Location structures Maintenance of Equipments Creation and maintenance of Maintenance Plans Management of SAP PM documents Ensure SAP PM master data is created and maintained in compliance with BASF standards and best practices , considering local regulations where required. Coordinate and guide SAP PM Data Operators to support the execution of operational master data tasks. Upload and maintain data in SAP PM using existing tools for mass data upload. Consulting, Standards & Governance Communicate SAP PM standards, rules, and framework conditions clearly to customers and stakeholders. IT & Tool Skills Confident use of Microsoft Excel and other Microsoft Office applications to support SAP PM service delivery and analysis. Ability to work with SAP PM-related tools supporting data maintenance and automation (as provided within the organization). Professional Experience & Qualifications Completed qualification as Master / Technician in process engineering, mechatronics, electronics, automation technology, or comparable professional experience. At least 5 years of professional experience in maintenance or asset management environments. Long-term practical experience working with SAP PM in an operational and consulting context .
