description:
GDS/CA - Solution Architect Device Security (m/f/d) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GDS/CA - Solution Architect Device Security (m/f/d) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Solution Architect (Job Grade 4) About Us At BASF’s Global Digital Services (GD), we are passionate about driving digital transformation in the chemical industry. Our dedicated teams deliver tailored digital products and services to internal business partners worldwide, leveraging the power of our Digital Hubs as dynamic centers of innovation and collaboration that bring us closer to our customers and regional needs. Here, you’ll join a high-performance culture inspired by our Winning Ways: enabling our customers’ green transformation, accelerating sustainability, and creating a workplace for curious, ambitious talents. In the Cyber Engineering & Identity and Access Management Product Family, we manage the digital identity lifecycle, offer BASF-wide standard authentication and authorization services, and provide solutions related to data and device security. Join our Device & Data Security Product Team and be part of a global community where your ideas and expertise make a real impact. Responsibilities Maintain BASF device security by planning, updating, and implementing cybersecurity measures and endpoint tools. Track and resolve vulnerabilities according to severity and business impact. Review and assess the effectiveness of current security measures and a dapt or develop solutions to address undefined issues efficiently . Report on security policy performance and suggest enhancements to stakeholders. Collaborate with cybersecurity teams, product owners, and other stakeholders for guidance and support. Manage tasks independently, coordinating across international and cross-organizational teams. Encourage teamwork, support colleagues’ ideas, and foster shared success. Share knowledge and best practices, building consensus between technical and business teams. Focus on operational excellence and bring new perspectives to problem-solving. Requirements A completed bachelor’s degree in computer science , i nformation t echnology, cybersecurity or related field is required, along with a minimum of three years’ relevant professional experience. We are focused on the modernization of legacy systems and development of new capabilities of digital solutions in the Operations Platform team. There is a growing global need within BASF to leverage digitalization to continue BASF’s journey of excellence and continuously improving our capabilities especially through digitalization. Main tasks You will provide operational L2/L3 support for Operations Platform (OP) applications in Ludwigshafen and other sites in the EMEA region, ensuring stable production operation. You will act as a key operational support contact for OP applications in production environments, supporting incident analysis and resolution in collaboration with Level 2/3 product teams and infrastructure support. You will support major and minor OP platform / application deployments, including: Deployment preparation activities Go live and hypercare support Post deployment validation and regional verification You will support incident handling activities for platform or application incidents, including verification, rollback support, and cross region alignment when required. You will collaborate closely with OP product teams for advanced troubleshooting, release support, and change execution, and with Level 3 developers for defect resolution. You will work together with first line support and external support providers by supporting escalations and follow ups until issue resolution. You will support technical and operational communication to sites and stakeholders during incidents, releases, and hypercare periods together with Level 2/3 teams. You will contribute to improving support knowledge documentation and operational practices based on real production issues and incidents. You will support other support engineers with technical validation activities and operational procedures. You will provide operational support coverage during critical periods, such as go lives, major releases, and periods of high request volumes. Job Requirements minimum requirement to carry out the work – you might want to point out “additional plus/nice to have skills” that are not mandatory Edu c a t i o n Degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, informatics, business informatics or in a comparable field Work experience Minimum 5 years of work experience in IT application or platform support , preferably in a production ‑ critical environment. Strong hands on experience with Azure DevOps, including deployment tracking, pull request, and collaboration in release and change workflows. Experience working with Level 2 and Level 3 product and development teams, as well as infrastructure support teams. Hands on experience with Service Management tools and processes (ServiceNow, ITIL framework). Experience collaborating with external support providers in operational contexts. Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills, with the ability to work under pressure. Good communication skills and ability to collaborate with stakeholders across regions. Highly motivated to deliver stable system operation and high quality user support. Self starter with a continuous learning mindset. Internal • Solid understanding of Identity & Access Management principles, including user lifecycle processes, role management, access governance, SoD, provisioning, and authentication/authorization concepts. • Proven expertise with One Identity Manager (design, configuration, development, and operational management). • Experience in writing technical documentation and functional specifications. • Programming experience in languages commonly used within One Identity Manager (e.g., C#, VB.NET, SQL, Angular or related scripting languages). • Ability to design solutions that meet business and company strategy. • Good conflict‑handling skills and a high degree of situational awareness. • In addition to the respective native language, very good command of written and spoken English. • Growth mindset, adaptability, and a passion for continuous learning and digital innovation. What We Offer • Be part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. • Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. • Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. • Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. • Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence [to include specific local benefits]. We are looking for experienced and motivated Controlling Professionals to join our team and play a key role in supporting our delivery organization from a finance and compliance perspective. This position is ideal for self-driven individuals who can independently manage end-to-end finance responsibilities and contribute to our growth story. Key Responsibilities and Main Tasks •    Conduct cost analysis, monitoring, reporting, and budgeting for your assigned business area. •    Liaise with the Finance Manager of the company. •    Provide actionable insights and recommendations to drive the future development of your business area within the BASF Group. •    Deliver reliable financial information to support management decision-making and strategic planning. •    Gain a comprehensive understanding of global accounting and charging mechanisms. •    Lead and support automation and continuous improvement initiatives within the team. •    Manage month-end and year-end closing processes, as well as forecasting and budget planning. •    Continuously improve and automate reporting to ensure transparency in cost development. •    Monitor project cost accounting and ensure accurate tracking. •    Prepare and deliver clear presentations to management. •    Coordinate management inquiries and structure complex ad-hoc analyses. •    Ensure compliance with BASF Group’s accounting and controlling guidelines. •    Conduct training sessions for stakeholders to promote smooth processes and effective knowledge transfer. Qualifications & Experience •    Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Controlling, or Accounting. •    Several years of relevant experience in business or controlling functions is preferred. •    Strong proficiency in controlling IT tools such as MS Office, SAP BW, SAP CO/CI, and PowerBI. •    Excellent analytical and numerical skills, with a strong ability to learn and work independently as well as in a team. •    Team player with a high service orientation and a proactive, continuous improvement mindset. •    Strong communication skills in English; knowledge of German is an added advantage but not mandatory. The team will be based in Hyderabad, India, and will form part of our new digital hub launching this year.  Its mission: to take over SAP S/4HANA topics transitioning from project mode into operations across multiple business areas—Finance, Procurement, Order Management, Planning, Manufacturing, Quality Management, Warehousing, SAP Business Data Cloud, and more. The team will work in an agile setup, closely connected to our global product organizations and to the ongoing global S/4 transformation program.  We are looking for a Product Manager who will shape and lead this new operational area from day one, starting no later than July 1, 2026. In this role, you will manage product activities as S/4 project waves enter steady-state operations, ensure value-driven and reliable delivery, and collaborate with stakeholders and peers across domains and regions.  This position offers the opportunity to build something new and become a cornerstone of the long‑term operational backbone of our global S/4 landscape.   Now is the time to take a new step: Own it. Drive it. Excel in it. Responsibilities   Build and lead a newly formed S4 AMS Operations team in India and additional regions, fostering an open, collaborative, and growth-oriented work environment. Enable the team to take ownership of S/4 operational topics transitioning from project delivery into stable operations, providing clarity, context, and decision-making support. Establish a robust operational model for SAP S/4HANA across multiple modules and business domains.   Drive continuous improvement of S/4 operational processes— leveraging AI where beneficial—ensuring harmonization, lean practices, and structured product lifecycle management with a strong focus on radical standardization.   Develop internal expertise to ensure long-term operational excellence across the S/4 solution landscape.   Take responsibility for business outcomes, operational performance, budget planning, and sourcing within the S4 AMS Operations scope.   Contribute to the organizational development of our broader product families and be an active member of a forward‑looking leadership team. Your role is focused on implementing the data strategy and enabling efficient, secure and compliant use of data across the company. Our goal is to make data easy to find, easy to use and safe – while supporting business value, innovation and AI use cases. You play a key part in federated data governance: governance is coordinated centrally but implemented close to the business. You will help embed data governance into daily work without slowing teams down. Your responsibilities You will facilitate and operationalize data governance across BASF, with a strong focus on collaboration and value creation: •    Coordinate data governance activities across different stakeholders •    Support the execution of BASF-wide data principles and governance guidelines •    Define, implement and continuously improve data roles and responsibilities (e.g. Data Owners, Data Product Owners, Data Domain Owners) •    Promote and support the nomination and onboarding of data roles in business •    Develop and share processes, templates and best practices for data governance across the organization •    Create transparency on data governance maturity, risks and improvement areas •    Facilitate data usage with clear and secure agreements integrated into the Data Catalog •    Identify, assess, and manage data-related risks, especially for AI use cases •    Support governance requirements such as data classification, ownership, quality, lineage and compliance-by-design •    Act as a connector between business, IT, security and compliance stakeholders What you bring •    Degree in business administration, science, IT or a comparable qualification •    Several years of professional experience in data-related roles (e.g. data governance, data stewardship, data management) •    Practical experience with governance topics, ideally in complex or federated organizations •    Structured and pragmatic working style in complex environments •    Initial experience in project or change initiatives is an advantage •    Strong communication and facilitation skills •    Ability to work with diverse stakeholders across business, IT and corporate functions •    Strong user and value orientation – you focus on practical benefits, not bureaucracy Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. description:
GDB/ZF - External data management specialist (m/f/d) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GDB/ZF - External data management specialist (m/f/d) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now External Data Management Specialist (m/f/d) As part of our Data Products & Semantics Team, you play a key role in the management of external data at BASF. You are responsible for ensuring that high-quality external data is selected, procured, managed, and made available across the organization in a transparent and cost-efficient way. With a current portfolio of 11 major external data providers, the team streamlines data demand across Sales & Market functions, with a strong focus on pricing, company, and trade data. You will work at the intersection of data, procurement, controlling, and IT, contributing directly to the standardization at BASF. Your responsibilities •    You take care of the external data management lifecycle, from data demand assessment, through purchasing and contract-related processes, to data delivery. •    You manage and continuously optimize the external data provider portfolio, ensuring standardization in line with business needs. •    You maintain and manage purchasing and work cycle processes in SAP in line with Procurement. •    You serve as the central point of contact for the external data portfolio, acting as the knowledge authority for subscription packages, licensing models, and permitted usage across the organization. •    You administer sub-services for external data subscriptions, including the creation, maintenance, and update of service calculations and cost models as well as general user access gatekeeping. •    You actively support the implementation of our standardization strategy, particularly by managing and evolving the charging model towards a transparent and standardized cost allocation. •    You are responsible for communication with data consumers and controlling stakeholders, including cost transparency, charging logic, supplier updates, disruptions, and invoicing-related questions. •    You collaborate closely with technical experts to ensure efficient data provisioning and management of external data as standardized data products. •    You develop a sufficient understanding of the underlying data and use cases to effectively support procurement in discussions with data providers and stakeholder related questions. •    You work cross-functionally with experts from data, IT, procurement, controlling, and business units across the organization. Your profile •    University degree in Business Administration, Business Informatics, or a comparable field. •    Strong interest in external data management, data provisioning, or data-related procurement processes. •    Structured and analytical working style with a high level of ownership and reliability. •    Working knowledge of Microsoft tools, especially Excel and Power BI and ideally first experience in SAP. •    Strong communication skills and the ability to interact confidently with technical and non-technical stakeholders. •    Nice to have: Familiarity with pricing data, company data, trade data, or market intelligence data providers Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. name: Sales Manager - Animal Nutrition, Vitamins and Carotenoids
description:
Sales Manager - Animal Nutrition, Vitamins and Carotenoids Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Sales Manager - Animal Nutrition, Vitamins and Carotenoids LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role To drive profitable growth of business in India. How You Will Do It Plan and direct sales activities in India to achieve agreed sales volume, pricing and selling cost objectives by implementing consistently the overall sales strategy, using technical, organizational and customer knowledge. Be responsible for business top line, bottom line and volume growth as per annual targets. Develop and maintain strategic long-term business relationships with respective accounts (all decision-makers) to promote brand value. Handle and improve sales of Vitamins and Carotenoids products in Animal Nutrition Market in India. Support Business Head, South Asia with inputs to efficiently steer business operations. Provide inputs and participate in the marketing, market planning and technical development of products and services. Update customer data, account plans and market concept of assigned customers and market. Provide accurate sales forecasts, supporting efficient planning of products and services. Work with the R&D, customer service and logistics personnel to establish a communication path with the customer to ensure sales opportunities for product and services. Develop market intelligence / Network to pursue market growth and deepen market penetration Nurture, grow and develop direct key account as well as Distributor Management to Optimize & strengthen distribution network and ensure effective distributors management. Collect and provide market information/requirements to the organization. Ensure proper cash flow and working capital management by achieving defined organizational KPIs such as DSO and DIV for ENV Animal. Develop lasting customer relationships to identify business trends in and beyond the customer scope or respective portfolio. Utilize product application knowledge as well as thorough knowledge of entire customer and market value chain to anticipate market trends. Develop a strong opportunity pipeline for existing / new products by identifying new customers, pursuing new applications, and interacting with corresponding process and product developers of the potential customers, leading to profitable future business. Identify customers to develop new concepts for our niche products (like Carotenoids) working together with our technical and marketing team. What We Look For B V Sc & AH with Specialization in Animal Nutrition / Bachelor of Science with experience in the relevant industry is preferred. Vet Dr. + MBA with relevant experience will be added advantage. 8 to 12 years’ experience in feed supplement & animal nutrition industry is preferred. Techno commercial. Techno- commercial individual with market & business orientation with excellent competencies of customer focus, communication skills, entrepreneurial drive & collaborating for success and ability to use MS Office, CRM systems (like Salesforce) and virtual meeting tools effectively. Work Location Any of the work location: Mumbai / Hyderabad / Delhi / Pune Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a

name: GDB/DH - Scrum Master (m/f/d)
description:
GDB/DH - Scrum Master (m/f/d) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GDB/DH - Scrum Master (m/f/d) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Summary Digitalization will be a true part of BASF’s DNA that creates new exciting customer experiences and business growth as well as drives efficiencies in processes. Global Digital Services is supporting BASF business in Digitalization transformation. Our mission is to drive forward the digital transformation of BASF, providing innovative, global and high-quality digital products and services through a strong agile culture. The Digital Hub Hyderabad is one of our global key locations to deliver regional and global solutions for BASF. Within our Product Family Development Solutions, we build up BASFs development platform that standardizes the app and AI development. We value reliability, trust and transparency and a great working culture. We strive for Commitment, Focus, Openness, Respect, and Courage on all levels. In your role as Agility Master within DevHub you will be supporting our scrum teams and their agile framework as a servant-leader, removing obstacles that may hinder the team's progress and support continuous improvement by encouraging feedback, promoting collaboration and adaptation culture to foster efficiency, and productivity. You will encourage teams to improve performance and maturity, providing the platform with an agile topology to evolve. Ultimately, you’ll help teams to be self-organized, flexible and fully productive during sprints focusing on delivering value to the customer. Responsibilities and Key Outcomes • Guiding and coaching the Scrum Team and DevHub in the effective use of Agile practices, creating an environment that enables teams to become self-managed and sustainably high performing. • Supporting and coaching the Product Owner by providing techniques to refine and prioritize the backlog, ensuring alignment with broader product and organizational priorities. • Assessing the agile maturity of teams and the platform and actively guiding them toward higher levels of effectiveness and continuous improvement. • Translating product priorities into clear team goals and actionable improvements. • Helping teams focus on high-impact work by reducing waste, limiting unnecessary complexity, and improving flow. • Adapting agile practices to the team context to ensure pragmatism, effectiveness, and continuous improvement. • Promoting strong communication and collaboration across the platform to support sound decision-making, shared ownership, and a common understanding of goals and agile ways of working. • Accompanying the Product Manager and key stakeholders in shaping the platform and supporting decision-making within an agile and, where applicable, scaled framework. • Identifying improvement opportunities and contributing to an agile roadmap together with the product family. • Improving meeting effectiveness through clear facilitation, purpose-driven collaboration, and a focus on outcomes. • Building a trusting and safe environment where challenges can be raised openly, with an emphasis on constructive problem-solving and continuous learning. • Supporting teams in resolving impediments, blockers, and conflicts autonomously wherever possible. • Facilitating discussions, decision-making, and conflict resolution, while supporting transparent and effective communication within and beyond the team. • Collaborating closely with other Scrum Masters and Agile Masters to share learnings, align practices, and improve consistency across teams. • Supporting agile transformation at team and product-family level. Job Requirements minimum requirement to carry out the work – you might want to point out “additional plus/nice to have skills” that are not mandatory Edu c a t i o n and work experience Bachelor Degree in Computer Science / Computer Engineering / Information Technology or business-technology related discipline Min. 3 years of professional experience in IT and / or IT Consulting C ertification in agile methodologies Scrum , LESs and scaled frameworks (additionally Kanban, SaFe valuated ) Plus: Professional coaching certification Plus: Certification in full stack tools ( preferably GitHub ) T e c hn i c a l & P r o f e ss i o n a l Knowledge (mandatory) Relevant experience as an Agile leader (similar position as Agility/ Scrum Master) Experience as Scrum Master for a team/s that was/were diligently applying Scrum principles, practices, and theory. Excellent coaching skills - high level of empathy and experience in conflict resolution Workshops/meetings/events facilitation. Experienced in international working contexts. English language by default . T e c hn i c a l & P r o f e ss i o n a l Knowledge (additional plus/nice to have skills) Trainer skills being capable to teach Agile, Scrum , and Sales Agile Frameworks content (Values, Principles, User Stories, Estimations, STATIK, Continuous Improvement, etc.). Additional knowledge in Kanban and OKRs highly appreciated Work experience in IT; specifically in development platforms Others D i r ect S ubo r dinate Position(s) Optional: Only for leadership positions Functional/Operational: NA Disciplinary/Local: NA Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. description:

Our dedicated teams deliver tailored digital products and services to internal business partners worldwide, leveraging the power of our Digital Hubs as dynamic centers of innovation and collaboration that bring us closer to our customers and regional needs. Here, you’ll join a high-performance culture inspired by our Winning Ways: enabling our customers’ green transformation, accelerating sustainability, and creating a workplace for curious, ambitious talents. Within Portfolio Steering, we ensure that BASF’s digital investments create measurable value. We provide transparency across applications and services, align demand with strategy, and steer the portfolio end ‑ to ‑ end—from intake and prioritization to roadmap and benefits tracking. By optimizing the application and service landscape, we also help identify redundancies, focus spend on the highest ‑ value initiatives, and enable cost savings while maintaining reliability and compliance. Together with product teams, service owners, and business stakeholders, we shape a balanced, efficient, and customer ‑ centric portfolio that supports BASF’s global operations and green transformation. Responsibilities Support and continuously improve portfolio steering processes for Business Applications and IT Services (intake, prioritization, planning, and governance) Support demand management by collecting, clarifying, qualifying, and structuring incoming initiatives and service requests (incl. value hypotheses and sizing) Facilitate prioritization discussions with business and IT stakeholders by providing structured input on value, risk, architecture fit, capacity, and financial constraints Maintain an integrated portfolio roadmap across products, applications, and services; coordinate dependencies and support alignment with strategy Support portfolio financials by contributing to budget planning, monitoring and cost transparency Help define and track portfolio KPIs (value, delivery, quality, cost, and risk) and contribute to management reporting and decision materials for governance bodies Support transparency of service performance and lifecycle by aligning with service owners on SLAs, lifecycle discussions, and continuous improvement initiatives Serve as an interface between business, architecture, security/compliance, and delivery teams by supporting end ‑ to ‑ end alignment and decision readiness Help prepare and moderate portfolio governance meetings (e.g., portfolio reviews, investment committees) and support follow ‑ up of decisions and action items Help establish and maintain portfolio standards (status model, reporting, KPI’s, data model) and enable teams to apply them consistently Support early identification of portfolio risks, constraints, and compliance topics and help coordinate mitigation and escalations with the right stakeholders Contribute to target operating model discussions for applications and services (roles, lifecycle, sourcing) and support implementation of agreed improvements Contribute to continuous improvement and (where possible) automation in portfolio reporting (e.g., dashboards) and support a data ‑ driven decision culture Support senior management communication by providing clear summaries, options, and trade ‑ offs to enable fast, transparent decisions Requirements Completed university degree in Business Administration, (Business) Informatics, Industrial Engineering, or a comparable field, with several years of experience in IT portfolio management, demand management, or IT service/product management Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to moderate discussions, handle conflicts, and drive decisions across business and IT Very good command of written and spoken English; German is a plus depending on the location Solid understanding of business applications and IT services, incl. basic architecture concepts, IT security/compliance considerations, and application & service lifecycle management Experience with portfolio planning methods (e.g., OKRs, roadmapping, WSJF/value-based prioritization) and agile ways of working; familiarity with Azure DevOps is a plus Good knowledge of IT service management processes (ITIL) and practical experience with ServiceNow & LeanIX for demand, service, and reporting workflows; ServiceNow/LeanIX customizing experience is a plus. Development skills (e.g., scripting, integrations, automation) are a plus as well—candidates can also develop these capabilities on the job. Strong analytical skills and affinity for data (KPIs, dashboards, financials); proficiency in PowerPoint/Excel and experience with BI tools (e.g., Power BI) is a plus High level of self-management, structured working style, and the ability to handle multiple topics in parallel in a complex, international environment Ability to communicate and present complex topics to diverse audiences, translating between business needs and IT realities Experience in steering external partners/ and a good understanding of commercial/contractual levers (SLAs, KPIs) is beneficial What We Offer Being part of a global leader in digital transformation, working in a vibrant Digital Hub that values innovation, agility, and cross-functional collaboration. Shape the future of BASF’s digital landscape, contributing to projects that drive sustainability and make a real impact. Thrive in a high-performance culture that supports your personal and professional growth, offering diverse learning opportunities and career development. Enjoy a modern, inclusive work environment where your ideas are valued, and your curiosity and ambition are encouraged. Benefit from flexible working models, international networking, and a strong sense of purpose as we enable our customers’ green transformation and lead the way in digital excellence. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Head of Finance India will lead all finance activities for the Service Delivery Unit (SDU) in India, ensuring strong financial control, insightful business partnering, and effective delivery of finance services that support the SDU’s growth and operational excellence. This role combines strategic leadership, operational finance management, process optimization, and people leadership to enable data-driven decisions and alignment with global finance standards. Key Responsibilities Financial leadership & strategy Develop and execute the SDU’s strategy in alignment with global objectives. Serve as the primary finance business partner to the global Finance GBS community, translating financial implications of strategic and operational decisions. Responsible for budgeting, forecasting, long-range planning, and scenario analysis; drive financial discipline and performance. Operational finance & process delivery Manage end-to-end finance processes delivered by the SDU (e.g., accounts payable/receivable, closing cycles, management accounting), ensuring efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and high service levels. Identify and implement process improvements, automation, and standardization Manage capacity planning, workload balancing, and operational resilience for peak cycles Performance management & reporting Define and monitor KPIs and SLAs (closing time, accuracy, payments on time, etc.) Use metrics to drive improvement plans and communicate performance trends and risks Talent & team leadership Build, coach, and retain a high-performing finance team Set clear objectives, development plans, and performance expectations; foster a collaborative, continuous-improvement mindset. Ensure proper delegation, succession planning, and capability development. Governance & stakeholder management Maintain strong relationships with Regional/Global Finance, Tax, Legal, and business stakeholders. Ensure timely escalation of financial risks and provide recommended mitigation actions. Represent the SDU in global finance forums and ensure alignment with corporate policies. Qualifications & Experience Chartered Accountant / MBA Finance / CIMA or equivalent professional qualification preferred. 12+ years of progressive finance experience, with at least 5 years in leadership roles; experience in SDU, shared services, or business unit finance preferred. Solid exposure to FP&A, accounting, controllership, and finance operations in an international or matrixed organization. Proven track record of process improvement and implementing finance automation/ERP systems. Strong business partnering skills with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and translate strategy into financial plans. Excellent analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Demonstrated people leadership, team building, and talent development capabilities. Responsible for development of new products and adaptation of existing products and tests the same to ascertain conformance to customer requirements. Coordinate with Technical product managers and TSS and handle product improvement requests, line application management, customer complaints. Prepare paint sample preparation and lab batches as per provided details and complete shade matching of the same Perform the testing as per specification & review the results with seniors. Preparation of activity report and submission of report to Product Owner/Tech Service. Solves problems, examines root causes of encountered problems, and works out and provide scientific solutions to problems Standardization of existing products as per line condition at both line & plant by interaction with cross functional teams and visits to customer lines. Guide and train juniors /Trainees as per organizational matrix of the laboratory and provide support and input to coworkers. Ensure development report & Monthly report, Form live. EHS Management: Develop and Implement EHS procedures at Customization lab to maintain safe working environment. RCMS EHS representative for Customization lab Conduct walkthrough and audits in CL to ensure early identification of hazards and initiate timely corrective actions and preventive practices. Implement Safety, Health, and Environment system to subordinates. To ensure the safe handling of hazardous chemicals and adherence to PPE usage and the Work Permit system is properly followed. Compliance of process safety and environment code as per BASF practice. RCMS: Maintain training records and PPE records. M inimum Education and Qualification Required for the Position M. Sc in Chemistry or Diploma in Chemical/Polymer/Coating Technology with relevant experience Working Experience (min required exp.): 6-9 years’ experience in automotive coating development. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Automotive Coatings ingredients and formulation technology Shade matching skills, basic knowledge of test methods, instruments and application techniques, Robotic applicator set up, OEM line process flow etc. Skills in Root cause analysis, problem solving, team development shall be an added advantage Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. name: GB/G Learning Administration Analyst
description:
GB/G Learning Administration Analyst Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description GB/G Learning Administration Analyst LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About the Role The service involves Success Factors administration (LMS, Talent Modules), management of Learning Management System (LMS) and other talent management modules, ensuring that the system is updated and functioning optimally for user needs, provision of support and training for employees using the LMS, full fledge organization of virtual or in presence training events, administration of online training formats, training data maintenance in Success Factors from the publication of the event to the invoicing of training costs, reports creation, forwarding training participation fees to the cost centers of the training participants. Additional local HR services include adaptation of global/regional learning and development tasks/projects to the needs of the country. What's In It for You Be part of building BASF's new Global Hub in India, a flagship transformation initiative Work with a globally integrated team spanning Finance and HR functions Competitive compensation, benefits, and structured career pathways Access to BASF's world-class learning & development programs Inclusive, diverse, and collaborative work culture Hybrid work model (mix of on-site and remote) Key Responsibilities First point of contact for Content Owners in the U.S. and Canada in the Learning Management area to ensure the best service in executing their requests and advising on their needs. As a Content Owners advisor, organize and coordinate monthly LMS training sessions and communications, ensuring that all necessary parties receive timely information for its application at the site-level training Execute learning process requests: Manage the full cycle of scheduled items and offers, including posting in LMS, tracking registrations, managing pre-work, and canceling scheduled offers in compliance with the SLA (Service Level Agreement) Maintain employee learning plans in SuccessFactors LMS for the U.S. and Canada, responsible for central LMS data management, ensuring data quality. Order, prepare, and send all required training materials as indicated by the Scheduled Offer Update LMS attendance and initiate the chargeback process Identify pain points and recommend changes in the LMS system and processes to gain efficiency Vendor management and administration for signature solutions Ad hoc global and regional projects: testing, providing feedback, and representing U.S. and Canada requirements, regulations, and feedback Minimum Qualifications Education: Advanced student / University degree in Business Administration, Human Resources, Psychology, or related fields. 1 to 3 years of experience, preferably with HR or service experience and/or experience with global companies. Advanced proficiency in English for oral and written communications. Ability to work effectively in a multicultural, fast-paced global environment. High attention to detail and a commitment to data accuracy Preferred Qualifications Experience in a Global Business Services (GBS) or Shared Services Centre (SSC) environment Prior exposure to chemical, manufacturing, or industrial sector. SuccessFactors experience a plus . Technical Skills Microsoft Office; experience with SuccessFactors, Power Automate and Power BI a plus . Experience with Reporting & Dashboards and Microsoft Excel or demonstrated ability and agility to learn systems; SuccessFactors experience a plus. At BASF, we believe that great chemistry starts with great people. We foster a workplace where curiosity is encouraged, diverse perspectives are valued, and every employee is empowered to make a meaningful impact. Our culture is built on trust, collaboration, and a shared commitment to continuous growth - both for the business and for the individuals within it. At BASF, your development never stands still. Employees have access to structured learning pathways, international exposure, and cross-functional mobility across a truly global organization. We are committed to inclusion and equal opportunity, and we actively invest in the wellbeing, development, and long-term career growth of our people. Whether you are early in your career or bringing deep expertise, BASF offers the scale of a world-leading company with the sense of purpose and community that makes it a place where people genuinely thrive. How to Apply Please apply via the BASF careers portal at www.basf.com/careers with your updated CV and a brief cover letter. name: RnD Logistics Associate
location: Navi Mumbai - HO (Innovation Campus) Country: India
description: Job Description: MAIN TASK: Import & export of shipments for chemicals and instruments/ equipment’s Preparation of shipment documents such as invoice, packaging list, SDS, CoA etc. Define logistics means in line with guidelines for e.g., IATA, IMDG Coordinating with import/export team for HS codes and clearing agents for customs for seamless operations Intimating consignee/forwarders regarding shipments import/exports Continuously monitoring shipments from point of receipts through to delivery Keeping records of all import/export shipments Keeping records of TSE/BSE certificates for chemicals shipment to USA Staying in continuous contact with APR/HP team for implementing shipment SoP’s as per requirement of APR unit & implement changes as & when defined PR creations for chemicals, consumables , instruments, equipment, AMC, capex items Ensuring compliance with EHS, Product Safety & Regulations, dual use technology, transport management. Act as a transportation & distribution safety authorized responsible person Understanding & implementing ERP and supply chain system management Maintaining agreements for logistics activities e.g., export, import, local shipments etc. a. Supporting internal/external audits related to logistics KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, and EXPERIENCE: Master’s degree, preferably in commerce, with 5 to 10 years’ experience in logistics preferably in RnD sample shipment across globe Should be fluent in communication Proactive, result driven and entrepreneurial approach towards tasks CORE COMPETENCIES: Demonstrate Customer Focus Collaborate for Achievement Communicate Effectively INDUSTRY PREFERRED: CRO’s, Pharmaceutical and Agro Companies Chemical industry in India with Research and Development Job Description Footer:

name: R&D Inventory & Logistics lead
location: Navi Mumbai - HO (Innovation Campus) Country: India
description: Job Description: MAIN TASKS: Legal compliance As a store responsible, ensuring legal compliance e.g., wrt to class A solvent storage, Poison store, Narcotics substances & solvent/chemical handling licenses – timely renewal of all related licenses and staying UpToDate with new guidelines/directives issued from time to time Performing periodic inventory safety checks & audits Store and inventory management Receiving and storage of chemicals according to their chemical classes Making Goods Receipt Notes (GRN) Issuing lab chemicals and consumables Implementation & troubleshooting of chemical inventory system (ERP) Coordinating with ERP team for any system breakdowns and implementation of new features Identifying near “expiry date” chemicals, informing to chemist & sending for disposals based on feedback received Review of inventory and raising orders for consumables & common chemicals/solvents as needed Training Prepare & implement training programs e.g., handling of controlled substances, disposal of “date-expired” chemicals, deletion of consumed chemical entries from databases etc. Conducting induction for new employees Providing ERP training to lab chemists as end users Managerial tasks Providing necessary guidance to logistics personnel and storage assistants Ensuring safety & compliance with local laws & company policies. KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS and EXPERIENCE: Master’s degree in science, preferably in chemistry, with 5+ years of experience in inventory management & logistics Should be fluent in communication Proactive, result driven and entrepreneurial approach towards tasks CORE COMPETENCIES: Demonstrate Customer Focus Collaborate for Achievement Communicate Effectively INDUSTRY PREFERRED: CRO’s, Pharmaceutical and Agro Companies Chemical industry in India with Research and Development Job Description Footer:

description:

We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About the Role : Asset on the development, execution, and operational activities involved in Talent Strategy across South America. Performs administrative and analytical tasks such as distribution and reporting to meet the Talent Services SA targets. What's In It for You Be part of building BASF's new Global Hub in India, a flagship transformation initiative Work with a globally integrated team spanning Finance and HR functions Competitive compensation, benefits, and structured career pathways Access to BASF's world-class learning & development programs Inclusive, diverse, and collaborative work culture Key Responsibilities Execute end-to-end learning administration activities, including publishing offerings in the LMS, monitoring enrollments, managing pre-work requirements, and canceling sessions when necessary. Maintain and update learning platforms and employee development records, including SuccessFactors LMS and related systems. Collect, consolidate, and analyze data related to talent programs, engagement surveys, and learning effectiveness to support reporting and decision-making. Support global and regional projects by conducting testing, providing structured feedback, and representing South America requirements, regulations and feedback. Execute and support ongoing talent programs and solutions in alignment with the regional talent strategy. Contribute to the design and implementation of new talent initiatives and solutions to address evolving business needs. Prepare, update, and enhance reports and dashboards within the scope of Talent Services to ensure accurate and timely information delivery. Support the translation of internal documentation, as required. Provide administrative support for vendor management activities, including coordination, follow-up and payments. Develop and edit internal communication materials related to HR, learning, and talent management initiatives. Build understanding of business unit needs and stakeholder priorities, identifying opportunities for continuous improvement in service delivery. Document processes, identify operational improvement opportunities, and support automation initiatives to increase efficiency and standardization. Minimum Qualifications Education: Advanced student / University degree in Business Administration, Human Resources, Psychology, or related fields. 1 to 3 years of experience, preferably with HR or service experience and/or experience with global companies. Advanced proficiency in English for oral and written communications. Ability to work effectively in a multicultural, fast-paced global environment. Exp with SAP, ServiceNow, Success Factors or similar platforms. Should be able to work in US shift Preferred Qualifications Experience in a Global Business Services (GBS) or Shared Services Centre (SSC) environment Prior exposure to chemical, manufacturing, or industrial sector. SuccessFactors experience a plus . Technical Skills Microsoft Office; experience with SuccessFactors, Power Automate and Power BI a plus . Experience with Reporting & Dashboards and Microsoft Excel or demonstrated ability and agility to learn systems; SuccessFactors experience a plus. About BASF At BASF, we believe that great chemistry starts with great people. We foster a workplace where curiosity is encouraged, diverse perspectives are valued, and every employee is empowered to make a meaningful impact. Our culture is built on trust, collaboration, and a shared commitment to continuous growth - both for the business and for the individuals within it. At BASF, your development never stands still. Employees have access to structured learning pathways, international exposure, and cross-functional mobility across a truly global organization. We are committed to inclusion and equal opportunity, and we actively invest in the wellbeing, development, and long-term career growth of our people. Whether you are early in your career or bringing deep expertise, BASF offers the scale of a world-leading company with the sense of purpose and community that makes it a place where people genuinely thrive. How to Apply Please apply via the BASF careers portal at www.basf.com/careers with your updated CV and a brief cover letter. name: Assistant Manager - Delegated Procurement
location: Navi Mumbai - HO (Innovation Campus) Country: India
description: Job Description: MAIN TASKS: Procurement of chemicals, consumables, solvents, accessories, safety tools, gas cylinders etc. End to end PR/Process operations Negotiations for competitive offers and fixing annual rate contracts for catalogue chemicals, solvents & consumables Developing new vendors, new vendor creation in system (IMDG) Tracking of orders & sharing status updates with stake holder Ø Co-ordinating with Controlling, Finance, EXIM, Stores & logistics; Analyzing CC / Category wise spending Handling vendor payment queries Tracking of open PO’s and timely closure Tracking import payments and supporting document requirements to ensure timely payments Supporting end users for capex procurement by obtaining budgetary quotes, CAPEX PR creations, Delivery follow ups for Domestic CAPEX orders, alignment with central team for timely PO processing, handling vendor payment queries Acting as a single of point of contact with central procurement team for annual maintenance contracts: Obtaining budgetary quotes, Tracking AMC's, PR creations; tracking AMC payments, handling payment & service related queries etc. Training Providing ERP training to direct reports Preparing procurement SoP’s, organizing training on SOP’s & guidelines to stakeholders Managerial Tasks Ensuring safety & compliance with local laws & company policies - Staying UpToDate wrt local laws and company guidelines e.g., dual use technology, adherence to Nagoya protocol Supporting internal/external audits from procurement perspective Understanding customer requirements, defining procurement strategies for RnD needs Staying within allocated budget and meeting defined timelines - Continuously striving to increase efficiency wrt costs & timelines Monthly spend analysis reporting Striving for digitalization to enhance efficiencies KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, and EXPERIENCE: MBA/MCom, with 5+ years of experience in procurement, preferably in R&D Should be fluent in communication Proactive, result driven and entrepreneurial approach towards tasks CORE COMPETENCIES Demonstrate Customer Focus Collaborate for Achievement Communicate Effectively Job Description Footer:

BASF Overview: BASF has successfully partnered India’s progress for more than 130 years. As of the end of 2025, BASF had 2,455 employees in India with 8 production sites and 45 offices throughout the country. The Innovation Campus Mumbai and the Coatings Technical Center in Mangalore are both part of BASF’s global technology platform. In 2025, BASF registered sales of approximately €2.2 billion to customers in India. Further information is available on www.basf.com/in About the Role: Analyze and provide useful information on inventories to mitigate significant impacts on product costs to business units What's In It for You: Be part of building BASF's new Global Hub in India, a flagship transformation initiativeWork with a globally integrated team spanning Finance and HR functionsCompetitive compensation, benefits, and structured career pathwaysAccess to BASF's world-class learning & development programsInclusive, diverse, and collaborative work cultureHybrid work model (mix of on-site and remote) Key Responsibilities: Perform the monthly valuation of inventories at standard cost and actual cost according to monthly month-end schedule. Analyze monthly variations of actual cost, standard cost, and production orders. Support in the definition and maintenance of annual operating plan that includes fixed production costs as well as variable rates. Perform inventory valuation adjustment. Calculate and account for provisions for low turnover, net realizable value and / or replacement price of inventories according to closing schedule Assist to communicate process deliverables to ensure all stakeholders are kept informed on progress and issues/resolutions. Implement continuous improvement of processes through new technologies and systems actively supporting the horizontal team. Minimum Qualifications: Graduates of Public Accountant, Business Administration, Economics Min 3 to 5 years of experience in relevant domain. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills. description:

We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About the Role The Senior Talent Development Specialist plays a strategic role in shaping, delivering, and evolving talent, leadership, and organizational development initiatives that drive business performance and workforce effectiveness. This role operates with a high degree of autonomy and influence, partnering closely with senior leaders and HR stakeholders to identify needs, provide consultative guidance, and implement scalable solutions. The Senior Talent Development Specialist brings advanced expertise in program design, stakeholder engagement, and organizational effectiveness, while also serving as a trusted advisor and coach to leaders and key talent. The role requires strong strategic thinking, executive communication, and the ability to translate complex business needs into impactful development solutions. What's In It for You Be part of building BASF's new Global Hub in India, a flagship transformation initiative Work with a globally integrated team spanning Finance and HR functions Competitive compensation, benefits, and structured career pathways Access to BASF's world-class learning & development programs Inclusive, diverse, and collaborative work culture Hybrid work model (mix of on-site and remote) Key Responsibilities Lead the design, development, and continuous improvement of talent development programs, initiatives, and processes aligned with organizational strategy Serve as a strategic partner to senior leaders and HR stakeholders, providing consultative guidance on talent, leadership, and organizational development needs Facilitate and influence decision-making through effective stakeholder management, including engagement with senior executives and cross-functional partners Manage complex, multi-faceted programs and initiatives, ensuring alignment, quality execution, and measurable outcomes Drive high-impact conversations with leaders to diagnose challenges, recommend solutions, and align development efforts to business priorities Coach and support leaders and high-potential talent to enhance capability, performance, and career growth Lead the creation of executive-level materials, including insights, recommendations, and presentations that translate data into actionable strategies Identify trends and leverage data, research, and benchmarking to inform strategy and continuous improvement efforts Champion initiatives that enhance organizational culture, engagement, inclusion, and overall employee experience Oversee and strengthen partnerships with internal teams and external vendors to ensure effective delivery of services and solutions Guide change management and communication efforts to drive adoption and sustain impact of talent initiatives Mentor and provide guidance to junior team members or project contributors as needed Ensure effective management of program operations, including planning, tracking, and resource alignment Minimum Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in Human Resources, Organizational Development, Business, Psychology, or a related field Approximately 5+ years of experience in talent development, leadership development, HR, or related areas Demonstrated experience partnering with senior leaders and managing complex talent initiatives Experience in consulting, coaching, or advisory roles within an organizational setting is strongly preferred. Skills & Capabalities Advanced stakeholder management and influencing skills, including ability to work effectively with senior executives Strong consulting and diagnostic skills with the ability to translate business needs into actionable solutions Experience coaching leaders and developing talent across levels Excellent executive communication and presentation skills Strong project and program management capabilities with ability to manage complexity Strategic thinking combined with execution focus High level of adaptability and comfort navigating ambiguity Data-driven mindset with ability to synthesize insights and inform decisions About BASF At BASF, we believe that great chemistry starts with great people. We foster a workplace where curiosity is encouraged, diverse perspectives are valued, and every employee is empowered to make a meaningful impact. Our culture is built on trust, collaboration, and a shared commitment to continuous growth - both for the business and for the individuals within it. At BASF, your development never stands still. Employees have access to structured learning pathways, international exposure, and cross-functional mobility across a truly global organization. We are committed to inclusion and equal opportunity, and we actively invest in the wellbeing, development, and long-term career growth of our people. Whether you are early in your career or bringing deep expertise, BASF offers the scale of a world-leading company with the sense of purpose and community that makes it a place where people genuinely thrive. How to Apply Please apply via the BASF careers portal at www.basf.com/careers with your updated CV and a brief cover letter. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objective: Responsible for ensuring reliable and efficient supply planning by translating demand requirements into executable supply and procurement plans in SAP/OMP. The role focuses on maintaining material availability, optimizing inventory levels, and supporting end-to-end supply chain execution through structured planning, workflow coordination, and cross-functional collaboration. Acts as a key interface between planning, procurement, logistics, and global stakeholders to ensure smooth value chain operations, manage supply disruptions, and support data accuracy and process improvements within a dynamic, international environment. M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities: Supply Planning: Develop and manage supply plans, schedules, and orders in SAP based on demand and inventory positions Convert planned requisitions into purchase orders, track order confirmations, and monitor execution progress Ensure material availability and optimize inventory levels through KPI monitoring and forward projections Manage obsolete inventory disposition in alignment with business guidelines Maintain and update SAP master data (planning parameters) to ensure data accuracy and system reliability Collaborate closely with global supply planners and cross-functional stakeholders across regions and time zones Value Chain Workflow Coordination: Process and record goods receipts in SAP to ensure accurate inventory and transaction postings Facilitate value chain workflows, supporting decision-making and process execution Transfer and maintain workflow outputs in SAP and other relevant systems Cross-functional Coordination: Collaborate with SCM, Procurement, and external partners to ensure smooth supply chain operations Proactively identify and manage supply disruptions, coordinating mitigation actions with relevant stakeholders Other Responsibilities: Monitor logistics costs (including filing, packaging, shipping, distribution, and demurrage) and track monthly trends Provide backup support for SCM team members during planned and unplanned absences Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives and cross-functional projects Prepare and maintain reports to support planning and performance monitoring Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology or Engineering, or Business Administration (any bg can be considered, 0–2 years of experience in Supply Chain or related field (or “relevant internship/academic exposure”) Proficiency in MS Office applications (Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) Knowledge of SAP systems (preferred) Strong communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate effectively with virtual teams and global stakeholders Excellent organizational and time management abilities to handle multiple priorities and meet deadlines High attention to detail with a strong focus on accuracy in planning and execution Adaptability and flexibility to operate in a fast-paced and dynamic environment Analytical thinking and problem-solving skills to address operational challenges Structured and process-oriented mindset with a focus on efficiency and consistency Fluent in English German language proficiency (mandatory) Apply now A unique total offer:
